title: RTX Corporation (RTX) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of RTX Corporation (RTX) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 84/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: 12% is a confident projection. While base defense spending grows in the mid-single digits, margin expansion is the key driver here. As the expensive GTF engine remediation program concludes, and high-margin aftermarket service revenues accelerate alongside global flight hours, FCF will expand significantly from its current $6.45B base.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: Why I'm Modeling Robust Growth for RTX
thesisParagraphs: RTX Corporation operates uniquely at the intersection of commercial aerospace recovery and escalating global defense budgets. Following the significant cash flow impact of the Pratt & Whitney GTF engine powder metal issue, RTX is poised for a massive free cash flow rebound. I see this as a powerful inflection point.

The record commercial backlog at Collins Aerospace and Pratt & Whitney, combined with Raytheon's critical role in modern air defense systems (Patriot, AMRAAM), creates unparalleled revenue visibility. As exceptional charges fade and production scales to meet demand, operating leverage will drive outsized cash generation.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 12% growth rate for RTX?
faq.answer: Gemini projects that RTX's massive commercial aerospace backlog, ongoing global defense spending tailwinds, and margin expansion following the resolution of the Pratt & Whitney GTF engine issues will drive strong free cash flow growth.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for RTX's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.0% discount rate was selected. This reflects RTX's remarkably low beta (0.406), a 4.18% risk-free rate, and the highly reliable nature of its long-term defense and aerospace contracts.

faq.question: What is the terminal growth rate assumption for RTX?
faq.answer: A 2.5% terminal growth rate is used, aligning with long-term GDP growth expectations and the stable, perpetual nature of defense spending.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict geopolitical events, supply chain crises, or management changes.

prospectVerdictDetail: RTX Corporation (formerly Raytheon Technologies) is a premier American multinational aerospace and defense conglomerate. The company boasts remarkable competitive momentum fueled by 12.1% year-over-year revenue growth. Supported by an impenetrable structural moat, entrenched government contracts, and over $10.5 billion in operating cash flow, its massive scale generates near-guaranteed long-term returns despite a complex $40B debt load. Sentiment remains bullish, reflected by high analyst mean ratings.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: RTX demonstrates outstanding momentum for a legacy defense contractor. Its 12.1% revenue growth rate indicates robust global demand across both commercial aerospace (Pratt & Whitney, Collins Aerospace) and military defense systems.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Achieving a 12.1% top-line expansion on nearly $88.6 billion in total revenue is exceptional. This growth materially outpaces many sluggish legacy industrial peers and reflects deep order backlogs.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: RTX is essentially an oligopoly participant. Along with Lockheed Martin and Boeing, it effectively dominates global aerospace and defense markets, guaranteeing consistent, large-scale market share.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Pricing power is mixed. While the technological sophistication of its products guarantees demand, many massive government contracts are fixed-price, shifting inflation and cost-overrun risks to the manufacturer. This suppresses gross margins to a moderate 20.0%.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The development cycle for next-generation jet engines, missile systems, and defense arrays spans decades. Product velocity is inherently slow, rigorous, and intensely regulated compared to consumer technology.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The structural moat surrounding RTX Corporation is practically impenetrable, forged by deep intellectual property, massive regulatory hurdles, and extreme switching costs across multi-decade platforms.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching costs are nearly absolute. Once a military adopts a specific missile defense system or an airline fleet adopts a specific Pratt & Whitney engine, moving to a competitor is financially, logistically, and strategically impossible for decades.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Traditional network effects do not apply to heavy industry. The utility of a defense system does not directly compound exponentially as more nations purchase it.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The national security imperative creates extreme barriers to entry. Competitors cannot easily replicate RTX's sensitive defense IP, classified clearances, or institutional relationships with the Pentagon.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Defense manufacturing is extraordinarily capital intensive. While the company generated over $10.5 billion in operating cash flow, massive ongoing capital expenditure is required to maintain manufacturing lines and research facilities.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment is highly positive. Geopolitical instability acts as a macabre but undeniable catalyst for RTX's core defense business, while a post-pandemic travel recovery boosts its commercial aerospace segments.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analyst sentiment is bullish, with a strong mean recommendation rating of 2.22 and a mean target price of $217.16, indicating confidence in continued backlog execution.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The geopolitical narrative is dominated by rising defense budgets in the US, Europe, and Asia. RTX, with its incredibly low beta of 0.406, is viewed as a highly stable safe-haven asset amidst global volatility.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management sustains a massive $7.4 billion cash position against a $40 billion debt load. Capital allocation includes a reliable 1.36% dividend yield with a safe 53.8% payout ratio.

prospectKeyRisks: Over-reliance on the United States Department of Defense budget, exposing the firm to political gridlock or sudden shifts in defense spending priorities.
Supply chain disruptions and localized labor shortages can severely delay the execution of its multi-billion dollar commercial and military backlog.
Technical defects or engineering flaws in long-term programs (like recent Pratt & Whitney GTF engine recalls) can incur massive liabilities and reputational damage.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Sustained increases in NATO and allied defense spending due to ongoing global conflicts and shifting geopolitical alliances.
Strong global commercial airline traffic continuing to drive robust aftermarket parts and service revenue for Collins Aerospace and Pratt & Whitney.
Aggressive execution of cost synergies following the historic 2020 merger of United Technologies and Raytheon.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored RTX at 81/100 and Opus at 83/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
